RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Wedding was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Even if plaintiffs had created a triable issue on that topic, plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Wedding's asbestos-related illness. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MOTION And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Trial Setting Preferenc Ccp36d
Off Calendar Oct-23-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Brian Maucotel of DEBLASE BROWN EYERLY LLP (310-575-9955), appearing for plaintiffs /
/ moving parties David & Lora Wedding
Brendan Tuohy of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / opposing party Schneider Electric USA, Inc.
(Refer to sign-in sheet for attorneys representing other defendants in the courtroom)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS DAVID & LORA WEDDING'S MOTION FOR TRIAL SETTING PREFERENCE

No argument made. The Court ruled as follows: Motion for trial setting preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(d). Trial is February 4, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is January 29, 2013. Plaintiffs stipulated that summary motions may be heard on 25 days notice without an additional two days for e-service. Time to respond to written discovery is 30 days. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than January 7, 2013. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on January 24, 2013. Fact discovery cut-off date is January 18. Expert discovery cut-off date is Feb 1. No later than Nov. 9, 2012, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than Nov. 9, 2012, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference on January 9, 2013 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5. All counsel attending the conference must have knowledge about the fact of the case, and be present with the representatives who have authority to settle. Any person(s) need to appear by phone must seek approval by the Court prior to the conference. The date of this conference will be determined by the Court. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 regarding the submission of the settlement conference statements. The statements will be kept confidential. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
